danger_signals.visual_form: Sprites moving toward player from screen edges
danger_signals.meaning: Combat threat; evasion or engagement required

safety_signals.visual_form: Empty sky with no enemy sprites or projectiles
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; opportunity to recover or reposition

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct collectible sprites (likely colored differently than threats)
Terrain or structure markers on ground layer
safety_signals.meaning: Objective location or safe zone boundary

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct enemy aircraft sprite designs differentiated from player plane
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy aircraft types with different silhouettes and behavior patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy airbases or ground installations as level objectives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dense enemy sprite spawning, heavy flak patterns, terrain features indicating militarized area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively pursue and attack; projectile density increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Medium enemy density, selective flak bursts, mixed terrain types
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol or patrol with intermittent engagement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy concentration increases toward level goal; environmental markers intensify
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defender density ramps; stronger enemy variants appear

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Objective structure destroyed or reached; screen transitions to next level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy spawning ceases; remaining threats flee or are eliminated

environmental_storytelling.example: Level backgrounds shift from friendly territory (clear skies, sparse ground features) to contested zones (burning buildings, active flak) to enemy heartland (dense defensive installations)
Different enemy plane sprites suggest opponent's air force composition and technological capabilities relative to player faction
Visual distinction between airfields, radar sites, industrial targets, and defensive positions communicate mission context without text

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Direct pursuit of player; coordinated attack patterns; aggressive sprite animations
Projectile bursts intensify as player approaches target; density increases with proximity to objectives
Visual tilt or sprite color change (if implemented); reduced maneuverability perception; increased vulnerability to further hits
Sprite animation loops for destruction; explosions cascade; visual collapse indicates progress toward mission goal

notes: Wings 2: Aces High is a horizontally-scrolling shoot-em-up with WW2-inspired aerial combat setting. Visual communication relies heavily on sprite differentiation (player vs. enemy aircraft), projectile density as danger indicator, and environmental features (terrain, installations) to signal world state progression. The shmup paradigm emphasizes immediate visual feedback for threats and opportunities. Faction identity is implied through aircraft design rather than explicit heraldry.
